title: schiedea hawaiiensis
intro: Schiedea hawaiiensis, also known as Hawaiian Violet, is a rare and endangered flowering plant endemic to Hawaii. It has been traditionally used in Native Hawaiian medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been shown to aid in wound healing and reducing pain. The health benefits of Schiedea hawaiiensis include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antioxidant properties. The plant is typically prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and salves for medicinal use, with some also using it in traditional Hawaiian ceremonies and rituals.
